name: Cardiac CT Coronary Angiography
description: CT coronary angiography for evaluation of coronary artery anatomy and stenosis

findings.technique: Retrospectively gated CT coronary angiography was performed following sublingual nitroglycerin and beta-blockade to achieve target heart rate.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Coronary Calcium Score
sections.content: The coronary calcium score is [0 / <10 / 10-100 / 100-400 / >400 Agatston units]. No calcified plaque identified.

sections.name: Left Main Coronary Artery
sections.content: The left main coronary artery demonstrates [no stenosis / <25% stenosis / 25-49% stenosis / 50-70% stenosis / >70% stenosis]. The lumen is [patent / calcified / non-calcified plaque].

sections.name: Left Anterior Descending
sections.content: The LAD demonstrates [no stenosis / <25% stenosis / 25-49% stenosis / 50-70% stenosis / >70% stenosis]. The vessel is [dominant / non-dominant].

sections.name: Left Circumflex
sections.content: The LCx demonstrates [no stenosis / <25% stenosis / 25-49% stenosis / 50-70% stenosis / >70% stenosis]. The marginal branches are [patent / normal].

sections.name: Right Coronary Artery
sections.content: The RCA demonstrates [no stenosis / <25% stenosis / 25-49% stenosis / 50-70% stenosis / >70% stenosis]. The vessel is [dominant / codominant].

sections.name: Cardiac Chambers
sections.content: The left ventricle demonstrates [normal wall thickness / no hypertrophy / normal cavity size]. The right ventricle is [normal / no enlargement].

sections.name: Myocardium
sections.content: The myocardial tissue demonstrates [no late enhancement / no perfusion defect / no abnormality]. No abnormal wall motion.

sections.name: Pericardium
sections.content: The pericardium is [unremarkable / no effusion / no thickening / no constriction].

sections.name: Valves
sections.content: The aortic and mitral valves demonstrate [no calcification / no stenosis / no regurgitation]. The valve areas are [normal].

impression:
1. Normal CT coronary angiography.
2. No coronary artery stenosis >50% identified.
3. Coronary calcium score of [X] Agatston units.
author: RadReport Templates
